title: laelia anceps subsp. sanderiana
intro: Laelia anceps subsp. sanderiana, also known as the Sander's Pansy Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The Sander's Pansy Orchid has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
